title: Polymorphism, Protocols, and Abstract Contracts · Behavior Case: Oop Typing Protocols
summary: Support interchangeable storage, notification, pricing, and item-policy implementations. Planned artifact: Add and verify the oop typing protocols increment in a protocol-driven circulation application. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define structural Protocol roles that match consumer needs and support static checking without forced inheritance.
Define abstract base classes with required operations and optional shared behavior when nominal hierarchy is justified.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
